name: Gaeng Kiow Wahn Gai (Green Chicken Curry)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bone and skin the chicken breast and thighs, resulting in about 1 lb of meat. Cut into large, bite-sized pieces and set aside.
2. In a medium, heavy-bottomed saucepan, warm the coconut cream over medium heat until it boils gently. Maintain a gentle boil, stirring occasionally, for 6 to 8 minutes as the coconut cream thickens. Add the curry paste and stir to dissolve it into the coconut cream. Cook for 1 to 2 minutes until the curry paste has an aromatic scent.
3. Add the chicken pieces and stir fry for 1-2 minutes to coat them evenly with the paste. Cook for about 2 minutes, then increase the heat and add the coconut milk, eggplant, fish sauce, sugar, and salt. Stir well. Add the coriander and maintain a gentle active boil, stirring occasionally, for 8 to 10 minutes.
4. Taste and adjust seasoning with additional fish sauce, sugar, or curry paste as needed. When the chicken is cooked through and the eggplant is tender, remove from heat and transfer to a serving bowl. Scatter basil leaves, chopped chilis, and coriander on top. Serve.
Preparation in advance:
Prepare up to the point of adding coriander, excluding the eggplant. Cool to room temperature, cover, and refrigerate for up to 24 hours. To serve, bring the curry back to a gentle boil over medium heat, add the eggplant, and continue with the remaining instructions. An alternative to the eggplant is Snow Peas; both work well.
